source: The Ovid Project, University of Vermont (Hope Greenberg)
license_note: Baur d.1642; 1703 print PD by age. One of the few UVM plates with a confirmed Latin label; UVa concordance matches it to Tempesta 54.
depicts: Pallas/Minerva transforms Arachne into a spider (the moment of metamorphosis).

plate: 54 — 'Athena Changing Arachne into a Spider' (Arachne in araneam a Pallade convertitur)
source: LACMA 65.37.138 via Wikimedia Commons (direct upload URL unverified — Commons API unreachable during research)
license_note: Tempesta d.1630 — PD by age; LACMA copy is PD. The Met's own Tempesta Arachne objects (401101/402120) are flagged no-image, so source the LACMA copy on Commons. FOLLOW-UP: confirm the direct file URL via a browser.
depicts: Minerva (armored) turning Arachne into a spider — same moment as the Baur plate.

_coverage_note: Solis (1581) and Salomon (1557) both SKIP Arachne entirely (their Book 6 runs Pierides -> Niobe -> Lycian rustics -> Marsyas -> Tereus). So for Arachne only TWO PD engraving plates exist (Baur 55, Tempesta 54), and both depict the same moment: the spider transformation. There is NO 'Arachne and Minerva at the looms' plate in any of these sets — a gap a modern CC-illustrator could fill (collaborator hook).
